# @samatawy/rules

> TypeScript utilities for parsing, validating, and executing declarative business rules.

@samatawy/rules is a declarative rule engine package for TypeScript and JavaScript applications. 
It supports a readable DSL for rules and expressions, declared types, custom and built-in functions, 
file readers, autocomplete support, rule graphs, runtime workspaces, diagnostics and logging, and testing support.

## Package

- Name: `@samatawy/rules`
- Homepage: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/
- Repository: https://github.com/samatawy/rules
- Issues: https://github.com/samatawy/rules/issues

## Machine-readable metadata

- package.json: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/samatawy/rules/main/package.json

## Documentation

- Full documentation: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/
- API index: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/modules.html
- Authored guides index: https://github.com/samatawy/rules/blob/main/src/help/index.md
- Engine guide: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/documents/engine.html
- Engine comparison guide: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/documents/comparisons.html
- Expression syntax guide: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/documents/expression.syntax.html
- Rules syntax guide: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/documents/rules.syntax.html
- Functions guide: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/documents/functions.html
- Declaration files guide: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/documents/declaration.files.html
- Diagnostics and auditing guide: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/documents/logging.html
- Testing guide: https://samatawy.github.io/rules/documents/testing.html

## Core topics

- Forward-chaining `modus ponens` inference engine
- Backward-chaining (goal-oriented) inference capability
- Workspaces, rule registries, type registries, and function registries
- A documented and concise TypeScript-like DSL for rules and expressions
- Rule syntax: `IF ... THEN ...`, `IF ... THEN ... ELSE ...`, `THROW`, and `SET`
- Expression syntax: arithmetic, comparison, boolean logic, arrays, lambdas, ternaries, and `SWITCH`
- Syntax parsers for rules, expressions, executables, functions, and types
- Top-down recursive expression parsing with operator precedence and nested structures
- Declared types and customizable type checking
- Input and output validation with configurable strictness
- Custom functions and built-in functions
- Built-in functions for numeric, string, date/time, boolean, array, lambda, constant, and random operations
- Rete algorithm implementation for highly scalable performance
- Optional dynamic compilation for faster repeated evaluation in supported runtimes
- Rule salience and conflict detection/resolution
- Detection of cyclic dependencies
- Working memory, scope context, and function-local execution scope
- Built-in audit trail, diagnostic logging, and stopwatch-style performance profiling
- Test suites, forward and backward test cases, and DSL-backed testing support
- Autocompletion support for editors
- File readers for rules, functions, constants, types, markdown, mixed/general files, and multi-file workspace loading
- Markdown-backed executable documentation
- Rendering helpers for HTML and Mermaid output
- Browser-compatible ESM runtime and Node.js support
- Typed public API with declaration files and preserved doc comments for IntelliSense

## Main components

- `WorkSpace` for loading declarations, creating contexts, and processing rules
- `RulesEngine` as a higher-level engine entry point
- `WorkingMemory` for inputs, outputs, exceptions, and execution logs
- `RuleParser`, `ExpressionParser`, `ExecutableParser`, `FunctionParser`, and `TypeParser`
- `RuleRegistry`, `FunctionRegistry`, `TypeRegistry`, and `WorkspaceTypeChecker`
- `RuleGraph` and graph nodes for efficient applicability traversal
- `Logger`, `ContextLogger`, `Stopwatch`, and Node file logger helpers for diagnostics
- `TestSuite`, `ForwardTestCase`, `BackwardTestCase`, and `TestsFileReader` for automated rule testing
- `Autocomplete` for editor suggestion support

## File and declaration workflows

- Supports direct code-based declaration through parser classes
- Supports constants, functions, types, and rules in separate files
- Supports mixed declaration files through a general reader
- Supports markdown files with executable code blocks
- Supports workspace-level loading across multiple files

## Entry points

- Main Node-oriented runtime: `@samatawy/rules` -> `dist/node/index.js`, `dist/node/index.cjs`, `dist/node/index.d.ts`
- Browser runtime: `@samatawy/rules/browser` -> `dist/browser/index.js`, `dist/browser/index.cjs`, `dist/browser/index.d.ts`
- Testing helpers: `@samatawy/rules/testing` -> `dist/testing/index.js`, `dist/testing/index.cjs`, `dist/testing/index.d.ts`
- Autocomplete helpers: `@samatawy/rules/autocomplete` -> `dist/autocomplete/index.js`, `dist/autocomplete/index.cjs`, `dist/autocomplete/index.d.ts`
- Rendering helpers: `@samatawy/rules/render` -> `dist/rendering/index.js`, `dist/rendering/index.cjs`, `dist/rendering/index.d.ts`

## Notes for LLMs

- Prefer the GitHub Pages documentation for the latest published API and guide pages.
- Use `package.json` for package metadata, exports, version, and runtime support details.
- Use the README for a concise overview and quick-start example.
- Use the authored help pages for syntax explanations and conceptual guidance.
- Use the Typedoc API pages for exported classes, methods, and interfaces.
- Prefer `@samatawy/rules/testing` for test-suite APIs instead of assuming they come from the main entry point.
- Prefer the diagnostics guides for audit trail, logger, stopwatch, and Node file logging behavior.
- The package is intended for Node.js and browser applications that use a bundler.
- The package focuses on deterministic business-rule execution rather than probabilistic AI workflows.
